The View Lounge truly lives up to its name. Located high above downtown San Francisco, it offers sweeping views of the skyline and the bay that are especially beautiful in the evening when the city lights come alive. The atmosphere is relaxed but still feels special, making it a great place to enjoy a cocktail while taking in one of the best panoramic views in the city.

A few of us from The Green Cross staff stopped in while we were downtown and it ended up being one of those memorable San Francisco moments. Watching the sun set over the city from up here is something visitors and locals alike will appreciate. Definitely worth stopping by if you want a drink with a view.

This place gets three stars only bc of Eva.  She was our server and very delightful, and very attentive to us. 

The place itself is meh. Was told to come here for the views and it sure had nice ones, IF you can get a table; we ended up in the back area which was nice in its own way bc there it was a quiet area with privacy.  This place is pretty packed with a younger crowd that was willing to pay for overpriced drinks; but that was expected as it’s a hotel.  $25-$28 is the average for a specialty cocktail, and they were all very meh as well.  Pretty drinks, but all doused with too much sugar and syrup; we barely finished and made our way out sooner than later.  

Some tips if you want to visit: maybe go at lunchtime or on a weekday?  Also, stick to your usual cocktails, don’t order from the specialty menu which offered lots of sugary stuff. 

I’m not cheap, but my overall review is that I just think there are better places to go for a cocktail that is less costly, with a view.

The view is truly the highlight of this lounge, offering breathtaking sights of the city. However, when it comes to the drinks and food, it feels a bit overpriced. While the drinks are visually appealing, paying over $30 for a cocktail seems a bit steep. I ordered the scallops, which unfortunately were not up to par—just two pieces that were quite chewy, and it's disappointing to find less-than-fresh seafood in a city like San Francisco. Next time, I would probably just stick to enjoying one drink while taking in the view rather than dining here.
